result.stdout:
[09:49:01 INF] AElf Cli (https://aelf.io)
[09:49:01 INF] 
Usage:

    aelf <command> <target> [options]

Command List:

    > help: Show command line help. Write ` aelf help <command> `
    > start: Start an AElf blockchain node on local machine.
    > accounts: Manage local aelf blockchain accounts.
    > create 
    > new: Generates a new contract development solution based on the AElf Boilerplate templates.
    > call: Call a read-only method on a contract.
    > send: Execute a method on a contract.
    > config: Get, set, delete or list aelf cli config.
    > faucet: Take test token from AElf Test Net.
    > oasis: AElf Top Of Oasis.
    > deploy: Deploy or update a smart contract
    > token: Create a new token in AElf MultiToken Contract.

To get a detailed help for a command:

    aelf help <command>

[09:50:06 INF] AElf Cli (https://aelf.io)
[09:50:06 WRN] Config command is missing!


Usage:

    aelf config <command> [options]

    command: get, set, del or list.

Options:

    -k|--key:    The key of config item. (Supported keys: endpoint, account and password.)
    -v|--value:  The value of config item.

Examples:

    aelf config get -k endpoint
    aelf config set -k endpoint -v http://127.0.0.1:1235
    aelf config del -k endpoint
    aelf config list

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.aelf.io

[09:50:05 INF] AElf Cli (https://aelf.io)
[09:50:05 WRN] Project name is missing!


Usage:

    aelf new <project-name> [options]

    project-name: Common convention is to name a project is like YourCompany.YourProject. However, you can use different naming like YourProject (single level namespacing) or YourCompany.YourProduct.YourModule (three levels namespacing).

Options:

    -o|--output-folder: Specifies the output folder. Default value is the current directory.

Examples:

    aelf new YourCompany.YourProject
    aelf new YourCompany.YourProject -o d:\my-project

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.aelf.io
